memoryArtifacts.headline: John Ap-Adam, 1st Baron Ap-Adam
memoryArtifacts.description:
John Ap-Adam III, 1st Baron Ap-Adam (died May 1311) was created baron by writ in 1299.

The first baron served in the Scottish wars. On 22 July 1298, he served in the battle of Falkirk and was knighte …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John William Ap Adam於1265年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾貝福斯東時，他的父親Reginald ab Adam23歲，母親Joan De Knoxville19歲。他於1291年與Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1311年6月13日在英國英格蘭薩默塞特查爾頓享年46歲，埋葬在英國。

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Adelhard, which is of ancient Germanic origin, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + hard ‘bold, hardy’. Compare Ale and Alma 4.

variants.name: Ale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos Enrique, Cesar, Jose, Maria De Jesus.
Italian (Sicily; Alé): probably a variant of Aleo .

nameMeanings.name: Adam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Giuseppe, Santo, Giovanna, Nicola, Aldo, Baldassare, Carmelo, Carmino.
Italian: from the personal name Adamo, the Italian form of Adam .

variants.name: Damo
meanings: Italian: from a shortened form of the personal name   Adamo  .
Hungarian (Damó): from a variant of Domó, a pet form of the personal name Domonkos ‘Dominic’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John William Ap Adam was born in 1265, in Beverston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Reginald ab Adam, was 23 and his mother, Joan De Knoxville, was 19. He married Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone in 1291. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 13 June 1311, in Charlton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John William Ap Adam est né en 1265, à Beverston, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Reginald ab Adam, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Joan De Knoxville, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone en 1291. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 13 juin 1311, à Charlton, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

Er hat 1291 Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 13. ja.lifeSummary: Sir John William Ap Adamが1265年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアベバーストンで生まれた時，父Reginald ab Adamは23歳，母Joan De Knoxvilleは19歳であった。1291年にLady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstoneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1311年6月13日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトチャールトンで46歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John William Ap Adam(이)가 1265년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 비버스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Reginald ab Adam(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan De Knoxville(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1291년 Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1311년 6월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 찰턴에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John William Ap Adam nasceu em 1265, em Beverston, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Reginald ab Adam, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Joan De Knoxville, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone em 1291. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de junho de 1311, em Charlton, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John William Ap Adam родился в 1265 г. в местности бивирстон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Reginald ab Adam было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Joan De Knoxville, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone в 1291 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 13 июня 1311 г. в местности чарлтон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия.
